title: Secret prevention and response deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Secret prevention and response; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Secret prevention and response: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Moving a committed key into .gitignore or deleting the latest file makes the old credential safe again.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Assume exposed credentials are compromised, revoke and rotate them, investigate use and copies, then clean history and prevention controls without treating cleanup as rotation.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Assume exposed credentials are compromised, revoke and rotate them, investigate use and copies, then clean history and prevention controls without treating cleanup as rotation.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Secret prevention and response instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Assume exposed credentials are compromised, revoke and rotate them, investigate use and copies, then clean history and prevention controls without treating cleanup as rotation.
Moving a committed key into .gitignore or deleting the latest file makes the old credential safe again.
